claim: In enterprise automation, the critical bottleneck is not model capability but the simplicity of building the automation—if building automation is harder than doing the task manually, users will do the task manually
actionable: Design your automation platform so that describing a workflow in natural language is faster and easier than manually executing the task; this is the bar for adoption
source_title: Rebuilding IT From the Ground Up for the AI Age: Serval's Jake Stauch

claim: Workflows + databases is the correct abstraction for enterprise software, but traditional tools require manual effort to build and maintain, creating lag when business processes change rapidly
actionable: Keep workflows-on-database as your architectural primitive but use AI code generation to build workflows from natural language, allowing instant adaptation to changing business needs
source_title: Rebuilding IT From the Ground Up for the AI Age: Serval's Jake Stauch

claim: AI-generated automation can produce duplicates and confusion if not managed—deploy an admin-layer agent that has contextual awareness of all existing workflows and recommends consolidation or improvement
actionable: Build a meta-agent that understands the full workflow inventory and suggests consolidations when users request similar automations, preventing workflow sprawl
source_title: Rebuilding IT From the Ground Up for the AI Age: Serval's Jake Stauch

claim: Product moat in AI comes from customer insight and empathy (understanding what customers need) not from product features which are easily copied overnight
actionable: Spend extreme time embedded with customers (being in all customer Slack channels, attending calls daily) to understand needs at a depth competitors won't match
source_title: Rebuilding IT From the Ground Up for the AI Age: Serval's Jake Stauch

claim: The product should focus on boundaries and controls rather than raw model capabilities—enterprise adoption hinges on security, permissions, approvals, and audit trails that limit risk
actionable: Build security-first architecture: permissions, approvals, audit logs, and visibility are primary product features, not afterthoughts; these create the trust needed for enterprise deployment
source_title: Rebuilding IT From the Ground Up for the AI Age: Serval's Jake Stauch

claim: Use a two-agent architecture: an admin agent that builds and controls workflows with full reasoning, and a help desk agent that can only use pre-approved tools, creating safety through architectural boundaries
actionable: Separate admin-layer (unrestricted planning) from user-layer (restricted execution); only expose tools explicitly approved by admins to end-user agents
source_title: Rebuilding IT From the Ground Up for the AI Age: Serval's Jake Stauch

claim: Different models excel at different tasks—OpenAI models perform best for user-facing interaction and tool calling, while Anthropic models (Sonnet, Opus) perform best for code generation and automation
actionable: Use OpenAI models for end-user chat interfaces and tool selection; use Anthropic models for backend code generation and automation tasks; benchmark both for your specific use cases
source_title: Rebuilding IT From the Ground Up for the AI Age: Serval's Jake Stauch

claim: New model releases are not plug-and-play—prompt tuning and infrastructure built around previous models breaks with new releases, requiring significant re-tuning before production rollout
actionable: After each major model release, run full eval suites and expect 2-4 weeks of re-tuning; sometimes downgrading to older models is better than deploying newer models that misbehave unpredictably
source_title: Rebuilding IT From the Ground Up for the AI Age: Serval's Jake Stauch

claim: Model vendor consolidation is unlikely because foundation labs derive more revenue from their base products than from building enterprise applications like ITSM on top
actionable: Do not fear major foundation model companies building competing products in your category; they likely have better ROI elsewhere and lack organizational focus to dominate enterprise verticals
source_title: Rebuilding IT From the Ground Up for the AI Age: Serval's Jake Stauch

claim: Implementation complexity scales with organization size not because the product changes but because decision-making power is distributed across committees in large enterprises
actionable: For large enterprise customers, budget 2-3x implementation time and build dedicated deployment engineer capacity to coordinate across multiple stakeholders and approval layers
source_title: Rebuilding IT From the Ground Up for the AI Age: Serval's Jake Stauch

claim: Unit economics improve when automation output is reusable code (not re-generated each time) because cost is front-loaded during generation, then near-zero per execution
actionable: Architect platforms so AI generates durable artifacts (code, workflows) once, then executes deterministically; avoid re-running expensive LLM calls for repeated tasks
source_title: Rebuilding IT From the Ground Up for the AI Age: Serval's Jake Stauch

claim: Organizational flexibility matters more than feature perfection in AI era—products may need complete rebuilds every 6 months as capabilities and markets shift, requiring small agile teams
actionable: Hire for talent density not headcount; maintain flat org with fewest-best hires so you can pivot without reorganization; avoid rigid role definitions and clear career paths early
source_title: Rebuilding IT From the Ground Up for the AI Age: Serval's Jake Stauch

claim: AI adoption in enterprise hits tension between individual user desire for autonomy and organizational security/control requirements—this gap is a key design challenge for enterprise tools
actionable: Design AI tools with clear permission boundaries; enable power users while maintaining org-level controls; treat security as a feature, not a limitation
source_title: Rebuilding IT From the Ground Up for the AI Age: Serval's Jake Stauch

claim: People remain the only durable moat—with accelerating change, the best teams execute 10x faster than average teams, making talent density the primary competitive advantage
actionable: Invest heavily in hiring and retention of top talent; make culture and team composition your primary competitive focus; maintain high bar on every hire
source_title: Rebuilding IT From the Ground Up for the AI Age: Serval's Jake Stauch

claim: Solutions engineering roles become obsolete when AI can answer customer questions instantly and build materials on-demand during calls
actionable: Empower AEs with AI access to answer product questions, build decks and comparison sheets in real-time; reduce or eliminate SE headcount as AI capabilities mature
source_title: Rebuilding IT From the Ground Up for the AI Age: Serval's Jake Stauch
